prompt: A clean flat illustration for a Japanese pest control blog. Shows a large Japanese giant hornet nest on a house eave, with Japanese yen coin icons and the text '15,000〜50,000円' displayed. Color palette: green and orange. Wide 16:9 format, minimal and modern infographic style.

A clean Japanese infographic showing hornet nest removal cost by size. Three sections side by side: Small nest (fist-sized) = 15,000-25,000 yen, Medium nest (volleyball-sized) = 25,000-40,000 yen, Large nest (bigger) = 40,000-80,000+ yen. Each with a simple nest illustration. Green and orange color palette, flat design, Japanese text labels, 16:9 wide format.

A Japanese infographic with 4 icons showing reasons why hornet extermination costs increase. Layout: 2x2 grid. Icon 1: calendar (nest growth season July-October). Icon 2: moon (nighttime surcharge). Icon 3: trash bag (disposal fee). Icon 4: map pin (regional price difference). Green and orange flat design, Japanese text labels, 16:9 wide format.

A Japanese comparison infographic: reliable pest control companies vs untrustworthy ones. Left column (green, checkmarks): written estimate, explains extra fees upfront, 1-year guarantee. Right column (red, X marks): verbal only, pressures you to decide quickly, no address listed. Clean flat design, Japanese text, 16:9 wide format.

A simple Japanese flowchart: 'Should you call a professional or DIY hornet removal?' Start → Is nest bigger than a fist? YES → Call a professional. NO → Is nest inside the house? YES → Call a professional. NO → Does anyone have allergies? YES → Call a professional. NO → DIY may be possible. Green and orange colors, clean flat style, Japanese text labels, 16:9 wide format.

A Japanese step-by-step infographic showing 3 emergency actions when you find a hornet nest. Step 1: Stay 3 meters away (arrow icon). Step 2: Remove black clothing (shirt icon with X). Step 3: Close all windows (window icon). Horizontal 3-step layout, green and orange flat design, Japanese text labels, 16:9 wide format.
